𝗙𝗥𝗘𝗦𝗛 | To provide a powerful, fast, and user-friendly text editing experience directly within the terminal, suitable for developers who prefer a terminal-based workflow or need to handle large files efficiently.  🔥 Super Hot!

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a powerful, fast, and user-friendly text editing experience directly within the terminal, suitable for developers who prefer a terminal-based workflow or need to handle large files efficiently.
📅 Created: Dec 2024 • Updated: Feb 2026

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Zero configuration
✅ Familiar keybindings
✅ Mouse support
✅ IDE-level features
✅ Command Palette & Fuzzy Finder
✅ Multi-Cursor Editing
✅ Themes & Customization
✅ Handles multi-gigabyte files
✅ Language Server (LSP) support
✅ Extensibility via TypeScript plugins


◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 6K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 211 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 99 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Fresh is a modern, full-featured text editor designed for the terminal, offering IDE-level features like multi-cursor editing, a command palette, and LSP support with zero configuration. It prioritizes performance, handling large files efficiently and providing a familiar user experience akin to graphical editors.

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/sinelaw/fresh

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

🔥 𝗙𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗼𝘄 𝘂𝘀 for daily GitHub projects!
👉 GitHub Open Source: https://t.me/GitHub_Open_Source

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇


🏷️ 𝗧𝗔𝗚𝗦

𝗜𝗡𝗧𝗘𝗥𝗖𝗘𝗣𝗧 | To provide a comprehensive and integrated platform for signal intelligence (SIGINT) gathering, analysis, and decoding using various software-defined radio (SDR) and related tools.  🌟 Rising Fast!

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a comprehensive and integrated platform for signal intelligence (SIGINT) gathering, analysis, and decoding using various software-defined radio (SDR) and related tools.
📅 Created: Dec 2025 • Updated: Feb 2026

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Pager Decoding (POCSAG/FLEX)
✅ 433MHz Sensor Decoding (rtl_433)
✅ Sub-GHz RF Analysis (HackRF)
✅ Aircraft Tracking (ADS-B)
✅ Vessel Tracking (AIS)
✅ ACARS and VDL2 Decoding
✅ Wideband RF Scanner
✅ Weather Satellite Image Decoding
✅ Remote WebSDR Access
✅ ISS and Terrestrial SSTV Reception
✅ APRS Tracking
✅ Satellite Pass Prediction
✅ Utility Meter Reading
✅ WiFi and Bluetooth Scanning
✅ Bluetooth Device Location Tracking


◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.3K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 162 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 14 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

iNTERCEPT is an open-source, web-based platform that integrates a wide array of signal intelligence tools into a unified interface. It supports various signal decoding, tracking, and analysis functionalities, making advanced SIGINT capabilities accessible.

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/smittix/intercept

𝗭𝗘𝗡 | A self-hosted, private note-taking and knowledge management application.  🔥 Super Hot!

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: A self-hosted, private note-taking and knowledge management application.
📅 Created: Jan 2025 • Updated: Feb 2026

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Single Go binary or Docker Compose
✅ Low resource usage
✅ Standard Markdown files, local SQLite database
✅ Organize with flexible tags
✅ Full-text search with BM25 ranking
✅ Import and export data with full portability
✅ Templates
✅ Pinned notes
✅ Archive and soft delete notes
✅ Automated backups


⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 56 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Zen is a self-hosted notes application that stores notes as Markdown files in a local SQLite database. It offers features like full-text search, flexible tagging, and data portability, all within a single, low-resource binary or Docker container.

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/sheshbabu/zen

𝗝𝗔𝗨𝗧𝗛 | Securing and managing access to self-hosted applications through a lightweight reverse proxy with flexible authorization methods.  🔥 Super Hot!

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐹 Go 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Securing and managing access to self-hosted applications through a lightweight reverse proxy with flexible authorization methods.
📅 Created: Apr 2023 • Updated: Feb 2026

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Single binary executable
✅ SSL/TLS encryption (self-signed or Let's Encrypt)
✅ User authorization via SSH or Telegram
✅ Optional Single Sign-On
✅ Whitelist-based access control
✅ Support for multiple domain names
✅ Minimal configuration


❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 169 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 8 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Jauth is a Go-based, lightweight SSL/TLS reverse proxy designed to secure self-hosted applications. It provides authorization through SSH or Telegram and supports Single Sign-On (SSO), simplifying access management for internal services.

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Jipok/Jauth

𝗚𝗜𝗧𝗝𝗢𝗨𝗥𝗡𝗔𝗟 | A private and portable note-taking solution integrated with Git for version control and flexible hosting.  🔥 Super Hot!

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📊 Dart 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: A private and portable note-taking solution integrated with Git for version control and flexible hosting.
📅 Created: Jan 2019 • Updated: Feb 2026

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Mobile-first note taking
✅ Stores notes in Git repositories
✅ Uses Markdown + YAML header format
✅ Supports GitHub, GitLab, and custom Git providers
✅ Focus on privacy and data portability
✅ Self-hosting options


⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

🍴 280 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 127 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

GitJournal is a mobile-first note-taking application that prioritizes privacy and data portability by storing notes in a standardized Markdown format within a Git repository. Users can choose to self-host their notes or use various Git providers like GitHub and GitLab.

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

𝗠𝗢𝗡𝗜𝗖 | Controlling external monitor settings (brightness, volume, input) directly from the system tray on Linux.  📈 Growing!

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Controlling external monitor settings (brightness, volume, input) directly from the system tray on Linux.
📅 Created: Jul 2025 • Updated: Feb 2026

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Brightness control with smooth animation
✅ Volume control for built-in monitor speakers
✅ Input source switching
✅ Multiple monitor support
✅ System tray interface
✅ Automatic monitor information updates


▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

🍴 4 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Monic is a Python-based utility for Linux that provides system tray control over external monitor settings like brightness, volume, and input source. It features smooth animations for brightness changes and supports multiple monitors with an automatic update mechanism.
